zh.title: Solana/美元技术分析：震荡偏多延续
zh.content:
当前SOL/USD维持窄幅震荡格局，价格稳定运行于80美元上方整理，整体呈现高位横盘特征。短线虽有上攻动能，但量能未明显放大，多空双方在当前区间内持续拉锯，趋势暂未形成突破方向。

技术指标显示，价格站稳SMA20均线79.81上方，短期结构保持偏多排列。MACD指标虽维持正值1.05，但动能柱未进一步扩张，说明上涨动能边际减弱，RSI缺失但从价格结构看未进入超买极端区。

下方关键支撑位关注79.80一线，其次为78.50区域；上方阻力集中在81.20与82.50附近。当前区间逐步收敛，若放量突破81.20则有望打开上行空间，否则仍以震荡为主。

交易策略上建议以区间思路为主，回踩79.8附近可轻仓试多，止损参考79.3下方；若冲高至81.2遇阻则可短线高抛低吸，等待方向性突破后再顺势跟进。

en.title: Solana vs US Dollar Technical Analysis
en.content:
SOL/USD is trading in a tight consolidation around 80.63 after a marginal 24h decline of 0.15%. Price action remains compressed between 80.51 and 80.79, indicating low volatility and equilibrium between buyers and sellers. Despite the slight pullback, the broader short-term structure leans neutral-to-slightly bullish as price holds above the 20-period SMA at 79.82, suggesting underlying support is still being defended and dips are being absorbed.

MACD is currently positive at 1.059, though the missing signal line limits full crossover confirmation. Even so, the reading suggests residual bullish momentum from a prior upside phase rather than fresh downside pressure. The absence of RSI data restricts overbought/oversold assessment, but the extremely narrow trading range implies neither momentum extreme is currently present. Overall, indicators favor consolidation with a mild upward bias.

Immediate support is defined at 80.50, aligning with the session low and the lower boundary of the current range. Beneath this level, the 79.82 SMA(20) becomes the key dynamic support and short-term trend gauge. Resistance sits at 80.79, and a clean break above this zone would open the path toward 81.20–81.50, where prior supply is likely concentrated.

Traders may favor range strategies while price holds 80.50–80.80, with longs near support and tight stops below 79.80. A sustained break above 80.80 would signal bullish continuation toward 81.20–81.50. Conversely, a drop below 79.80 would invalidate the short-term bullish bias and expose 78.50. Given muted volatility, disciplined entries and tight risk control are essential for positioning.
